name: Gooseberry Cobbler from Loren Martin
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, cornmeal, baking powder, and salt.
2. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Gradually add the boiling water, stirring constantly until a dough forms.
4. Divide the dough into two portions. Pat one portion into a buttered 8"x8"x2-inch baking pan and sprinkle with 1 tablespoon cornmeal.
5. Mash half of the gooseberries in their syrup, then stir in the remaining gooseberries, honey, and lemon juice. Pour over the dough.
6. Top with the remaining dough and sprinkle with the remaining tablespoon cornmeal.
7. Bake in a very hot oven (425°F) for 30 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned. Cut into squares and serve.
